scenario: A team uses Web Modeler in SaaS. They want to back up an entire project -- all BPMN, DMN, and Form files -- as a single archive (e.g., for compliance, off-site backup, or migration to another cluster).
question: Which Web Modeler feature fits "export entire project as ZIP"?

options.text: Manually open each BPMN, DMN, and Form file in Web Modeler, view the XML/JSON source, and copy-paste it into individual files on disk, building the archive manually. For a project with many resources this is extremely tedious and error-prone: files can be missed, the wrong version may be copied, manually tracking which files belong to which process application is difficult, and file naming conventions may be lost. The multi-file Download feature in Web Modeler was built precisely to replace this manual workflow with a single reliable action.

Web Modeler provides no file export functionality -- it is a cloud-based tool where all content remains stored in the cloud, and downloading files for offline backup or migration has no documented path. This claim is inaccurate. md) that supports multi-file and folder-level downloads, packaging the selected files into a ZIP archive. This capability was designed for backup, migration, and offline sharing workflows, and is a standard part of the Web Modeler feature set.

Use the Web Modeler REST API to programmatically fetch each resource file individually -- enumerate projects, list files, download file content for each one, and assemble them into a local archive. While this approach is viable for automation scenarios (CI backup jobs, migration scripts, integration testing), it requires custom scripting to handle pagination, file type detection, and archive assembly. For a one-off compliance backup, the built-in UI Download feature is significantly faster and requires no custom code. The API approach is appropriate when the backup must be automated and scheduled.

md:26 28 ("A folder will be downloaded as a zip archive containing all the folder's files and subfolders"). Limit per :33: up to 100 files and 20 folders at the same time. For larger projects, split into multiple folder level downloads or use the Web Modeler API for programmatic batch fetch.

a.text: Incorrect. Multi-file / folder Download is the canonical UI backup path per file-download.md:19-34. Select a folder (or multi-select files/folders), click Download -- Web Modeler bundles into ZIP. Easy to commit to Git, store off-site, or import to another cluster via the import flow (preparing-resources-for-import.md + importing-resources.md). For 100+ file projects, split or use the Web Modeler API (apis-tools/web-modeler-api/).

d.text: Correct. API-based fetch is good for automation (CI backups), but for one-off backup the UI export is simpler.

explanation: API-based fetch is good for automation (CI backups), but for one-off backup the UI export is simpler.
